item.title: More than a digestive disorder: Perceived stigma in inflammatory bowel disease patients.
item.description: PloS one - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic condition characterized by symptoms that are often difficult to conceal and may expose patients to negative social reactions. Beyond physical burden, s...

item.title: Resection of the mesentery vs Kono-S anastomosis in preventing surgical recurrence for primary Crohn disease: the REMEASURE prospective, randomized, controlled trial.
item.description: Updates in surgery - Many authors have investigated whether a new functional end-to-end anastomosis, the Kono-S, or the resection of the mesentery could allow better results following primary ileocolic-resection for Cr...

item.title: Endoscopic Autologous Mucosal Transplantation (EAMT) for Management of Chronic Non-Healing Luminal Defects.
item.description: Gastrointestinal endoscopy - Chronic non-healing gastrointestinal defects remain difficult to manage. Existing techniques for endoscopic closure rely heavily on mechanical tissue approximation and may fail when the surrounding...

item.title: Indirect comparison of endoscopic balloon dilatation, stent placement, and stricturotomy for lower gastrointestinal strictures in Crohn's disease: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: European journal of gastroenterology & hepatology - Meta-analysis - Outcomes of endoscopic balloon dilatation (EBD), endoscopic stent placement (ESP), and endoscopic stricturotomy for Crohn's disease-related strictures vary across studies. We conducted an indirect ...

item.title: Correction to: Preoperative phase angle and postoperative complications in Crohn's disease patients undergoing ileocolic resection: a retrospective cohort study.
Diversion proctitis during staged restorative proctocolectomy with ileal pouch anal anastomosis is not associated with subsequent cuffitis.
item.description: International journal of colorectal disease - The aim of this study was to assess the impact of diversion proctitis (DP) between the first and second surgical stage on pouch outcomes in patients with ulcerative colitis (UC) who underwent three...

item.title: Beyond the Intestinal Mucosa in Long-Standing Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Consequences of Chronic Inflammation and Endoscopic Approaches to Diagnosis and Management.
item.description: Medicina (Kaunas, Lithuania) - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) includes Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), chronic immune-mediated conditions of the gastrointestinal tract characterized by alternating periods of ...

item.title: Patient-derived tissue slice cultures from endoscopic biopsies as a translational ex vivo model for inflammatory bowel diseases.
item.description: Clinical and experimental medicine - Effective inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) management increasingly relies on patient-stratified therapeutic approaches, yet clinically accessible platforms for ex vivo pharmacological evaluation re...

item.title: Imaging-based prediction of biologic failure in perianal Crohn's disease: the emerging role of 3D transrectal ultrasound.
Clinical features and therapeutic requirements of perianal disease in adult patients with ulcerative colitis: the PERICO study of the ENEIDA registry.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Perianal disease (PD) may occur in ulcerative colitis (UC), but scarce data on its features, clinical course, and common therapeutic approach are available. Our aim was to describe the prevalence, ...

item.title: Exploratory Serum-Based Surface-Enhanced Raman Spectroscopy Analysis in Crohn's Disease: A Pilot Cross-Sectional Study.
item.description: International journal of molecular sciences - Crohn's disease (CD) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease requiring accurate and timely diagnosis. Current diagnostic tools may be invasive, costly, or insufficiently specific. Surface-enhanced ...

item.title: Effect of Short-Term Grape Powder Supplementation in Patients with Crohn's Disease: A Pilot Study.
item.description: Nutrients - Background: The overall objective of this pilot diet intervention study was to determine the effect of grape powder (GP) supplementation on gut microbiota composition and inflammatory markers in in...

item.title: Endoscopic characteristics and clinical management of cap polyposis: Insights from seven cases.
item.description: Endoscopy international open - Cap polyposis (CP) is an uncommon benign condition affecting the colorectal region. This study aimed to examine endoscopic features, therapeutic responses, and clinical outcomes of CP, with particu...

item.title: Dual Actions of Butyrate on Immunoepithelial Remodeling in Ex Vivo Intestinal Biopsies from Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Metabolites - Background/Objectives: Butyrate, a microbiota-derived short-chain fatty acid, plays a central role in intestinal homeostasis, yet its concentration-dependent effects on human inflamed mucosa remain...
